communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Florida Sunshine Law, I hereby request the following records:

All emails between Michael Gottlieb and Randy Fine from 8 November 2018 to the date this request is processed.

If any emails contain attachments, please include those attachments as well. If some portions of the requested records are properly exempt from disclosure, please disclose any reasonably segregable non-exempt portions of the requested records.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Matthew Petti

title: Chilean Sea Bass - Chilean Lumber Drug Trafficking Operation

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Chilean Sea Bass - Chilean Lumber Drug Trafficking Operation
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Subject: Freedom of Information Act Request – Chilean Lumber Drug Trafficking Operation

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, 5 U.S.C. § 552, I request records maintained by the Office of the Director of National Intelligence concerning the June 2026 seizure by Chilean authorities of approximately 100 tons of cocaine, ketamine, and other illicit narcotics concealed within lumber or timber shipments.

Specifically, I request:

1. Intelligence reports, Intelligence Information Reports (IIRs), finished intelligence products, assessments, situation reports, intelligence notes, analytical products, briefing materials, executive summaries, threat assessments, or similar records generated, received, reviewed, disseminated, or maintained by ODNI concerning the seizure, the associated trafficking organizations, transportation routes, source countries, destination countries, or related criminal networks.

2. Records reflecting intelligence sharing, coordination, information exchange, operational support, analytical support, or joint investigative efforts between the United States and the governments of Chile, Bolivia, European Union member states, Europol, Interpol, or any foreign law enforcement or intelligence service concerning the seizure or related trafficking activity.

3. Records identifying, discussing, assessing, or analyzing whether the narcotics associated with the seizure were intended for distribution within the United States or whether any U.S.-based criminal organizations, transnational criminal organizations, cartels, facilitators, brokers, importers, exporters, financial networks, or other entities were linked to the shipment.

4. Intelligence assessments, strategic intelligence products, technical reports, analytical memoranda, threat assessments, or briefing materials created from January 1, 2024, through the date of the search discussing the concealment of narcotics within lumber, timber, wood products, chemically impregnated wood products, or similar concealment methodologies.

5. Records provided to, received from, transmitted to, referenced by, relied upon, summarized by, disseminated to, or coordinated with the Drug Enforcement Administration (DEA), Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI), Department of Homeland Security (DHS), U.S. Customs and Border Protection (CBP), DEA Special Operations Division (SOD), Department of State, Department of the Treasury, Central Intelligence Agency (CIA), Defense Intelligence Agency (DIA), National Security Agency (NSA), National Counterterrorism Center (NCTC), or any other federal agency, Intelligence Community element, task force, fusion center, foreign government, international organization, or law enforcement entity concerning this matter.

6. Records sufficient to identify ODNI intelligence products, reports, assessments, dissemination records, briefing documents, or analytical products concerning the seizure, including title, date, originating office, classification level, recipient office, and distribution information.

Referral and Consultation Request

To the extent that responsive records originated with, were received from, contain equities of, or are otherwise subject to review by another agency, component, office, or government entity, I request that such records be processed pursuant to applicable FOIA consultation and referral procedures.

Please do not deny or administratively close portions of this request solely because another agency originated, contributed to, or maintains an interest in the responsive records. Instead, I request that responsive records be referred or consulted as appropriate for release consideration.

For each referral or consultation made, please provide the name of the receiving agency or component, the date the referral or consultation was initiated, any assigned tracking number, and a description sufficient to identify the records referred where such disclosure would not reveal exempt information.

Requested Action

• Conduct a search reasonably calculated to locate responsive records.

• Release all reasonably segregable non-exempt portions.

• Provide responsive records electronically.

• Provide a Vaughn index describing records withheld in whole or in part.

• Describe the foreseeable harm supporting any withholding pursuant to the FOIA Improvement Act of 2016.

Fee Waiver Request

The requested records concern a significant international narcotics trafficking investigation and intelligence activities relating to transnational organized crime. Disclosure is likely to contribute significantly to public understanding of government operations and activities. This request is made for news-gathering, research, and public-interest purposes and is not for commercial use.

Related News:
Chile seizes wood laced with 'historic' 100 tons of drugs
Dharvi Vaid with AFP
06/09/2026June 9, 2026
Chile has announced its largest-ever drug seizure. About 1,080 tons of wood were found laced with illegal drugs — mostly cocaine and ketamine — headed to Europe.
https://www.dw.com/en/snorting-sawdust-chile-seizes-wood-laced-with-historic-100-tons-of-drugs/a-77467556


Respectfully,

William Fernandes

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

William Fernandes
